typeSystemDesign.abstract: The E.L.I.A. language employs an extended orthogonal type classification model, based not on a single criterion but on a set of independent dimensions.

dataBehaviorSplit.abstract: The philosophical separation between Record and Class is enforced in E.L.I.A.

seq.H2: Persistence as an Explicit Flow Instruction (Normative)

persistenceDesign.abstract: Persistence in E.L.I.A. is not an implicit side-effect of execution, but an explicitly declared and enforced operation.

streamDesign.abstract: Streams are the essential mechanism for exchanging serialized artefacts both within a domain and across domain boundaries.

seq.H2: Error as a Type (Data, Not Behavior)
How Errors Settle in Logs (Application vs System)

errorDesign.abstract: Errors are treated as typed data artefacts that represent failure semantics, not as a behavioral mechanism.

eventDesign.abstract: Events are immutable facts emitted by a domain to represent that something *has happened*.

surrogateDesign.abstract: A Data Surrogate is a first-class data artefact that becomes *semantically visible* by attaching Explanation mechanics (and optionally...

ruleDesign.abstract: A Rule is an *authority behavioral construct* that defines deterministic semantic reasoning logic. Includes forward chaining execution model and evaluation sequencing.

actionDesign.abstract: An Action is an *authority behavioral construct* that declares a business-meaningful intent which may be executed as part of a flow once admitted. Includes admissibility matrix and linear model constraints.

dfdLayering.abstract: Domain‑Driven Flow Design (DFD) describes how domain intent, requirements, and risks are expressed as concrete data flows.

semanticModelDesign.abstract: Semantic model in E.L.I.A. is declarative and deterministic (consists of finite set of primitives).

classDesign.abstract: A Class is a *capable behavioral construct* that represents a concrete, state-bearing execution unit.
